behavior_summary: Provides gameplay handlers for a wardrobe item: a 'U8P_WARDNS::look' handler that composes and emits the short label "wardrobe " to the examining actor, and a 'use' handler that toggles the wardrobe's UI gump based on a status bit.
WARDNS::look prepares item/process state, builds the display string, calls Item::bark to present the text to the viewer, then suspends the script.
WARDNS::use reads the item's status flags via Item::getStatus(), tests bit 0x0100, and either calls Item::openGump(this, 0x0D) when the bit is set or Item::closeGump(this) when the bit is clear.
notable_control_flow: WARDNS::look is an event-driven entry (Event 0) that performs a single UI/message emission and then suspends; it does not change global state.
WARDNS::use branches on a single status bit (0x0100) read from the item and dispatches to one of two gump-related external calls (openGump vs closeGump).
No global flags are read or written by this script; behavior is localized to the item instance referred to by the incoming parameter.
open_questions: What exact semantic meaning does status bit 0x0100 encode for this wardrobe (locked/opened/alternate UI state)?
Is the pushed byte 0x0D to Item::openGump a gump template id or an argument encoding a view/mode? The disassembly shows 0x0D but its semantic mapping is inferred.
Are there other event handlers (e.g., enterFastArea/leaveFastArea) for this item not present in this fragment that affect wardrobe state?

main_function.name: U8P_WARDNS::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the player 'U8P_WARDNS::look' (examine) action on the wardrobe: set up item info/process-exclude, compose the label "wardrobe ", call Item::bark to present that label to the examining process, then suspend.
entry_conditions: Event 0 'U8P_WARDNS::look' — invoked when the avatar or an actor examines/looks at the wardrobe object

function_map.name: U8P_WARDNS::look
function_map.role: Examine/U8P_WARDNS::look event handler that emits the item label via Item::bark.

function_map.name: U8P_WARDNS::use
function_map.role: Use event handler that toggles the wardrobe's UI gump by reading the item's status and calling openGump/closeGump accordingly.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item routine used to display or log a short text (the item's 'bark').

function_map.name: Item::getStatus
function_map.role: Engine/item routine returning the item's status/flags word used to decide behavior.

function_map.name: Item::closeGump
function_map.role: Engine/item routine to close/hide the item's UI gump.

function_map.name: Item::openGump
function_map.role: Engine/item routine to open/show the item's UI gump with a parameter (0x0D used here).

external_calls.purpose: Emit/display the short label string "wardrobe " to the examining process/actor; used as the item's U8P_WARDNS::look/description feedback.
Read the item's status/flags word to inspect bit 0x0100 which determines the next action.
Close or hide the wardrobe's UI when the inspected status bit (0x0100) is clear.
Open/show the wardrobe's UI with parameter 0x0D when the inspected status bit (0x0100) is set.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides gameplay handlers for a wardrobe item: a 'U8P_WARDNS::look' handler that composes and emits the short label "wardrobe " to the examining actor, and a 'use' handler that toggles the wardrobe's UI gump based on a status bit.
